creator: erik@cederstrand.dk
text:
Ah, I figured it out. MySQL on this server has been compiled with GCC. I get this in the install log:

/usr/ports/databases/p5-DBD-mysql% sudo make
[...]
I will use the following settings for compiling and testing:

  cflags        (mysql_config) = -I/usr/local/include/mysql -pipe -fno-strict-aliasing -g -fabi-version=2 -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-strict-aliasing
  embedded      (mysql_config) = 
  ldflags       (mysql_config) = -pthread
  libs          (mysql_config) = -L/usr/local/lib/mysql -lmysqlclient -lm
[...]
cc: error: unknown argument: '-fabi-version=2'
*** [dbdimp.o] Error code 1
[...]


Sure enough, mysql_config emits cflags containing "fabi-version". So p5-DBD-mysql is getting cflags from a MySQL that was compiled with GCC and passing that to clang.

Not sure this is even worthy of a fix. It's rather pilot error on my part.
